proverbs.italian: Ambasciator non porta pena
proverbs.english: Don't shoot the messenger
proverbs.french: Ne tirez pas sur le messager
proverbs.meaning: Someone delivering bad or unpleasant news on behalf of others isn't responsible and shouldn't be blamed.

proverbs.italian: Cane che abbaia non morde
proverbs.english: A barking dog doesn't bite
proverbs.french: Chien qui aboie ne mord pas
proverbs.meaning: Those who make many threats often don't follow through with them.

proverbs.italian: Fidarsi è bene ma non fidarsi è meglio
proverbs.english: Trust is good, but being cautious is better
proverbs.french: Faire confiance est bien, mais ne pas faire confiance est mieux
proverbs.meaning: While trust is vital, it's wise not to be overly naive.

proverbs.italian: Chi si volta, e chi si gira, sempre a casa va finire
proverbs.english: No matter where you turn or roam, you'll always end up at home
proverbs.french: Où que vous vous tourniez, vous finirez toujours par rentrer chez vous
proverbs.meaning: No matter how far we wander, we will eventually find our way back home.

proverbs.italian: Finché c'è vita, c'è speranza
proverbs.english: As long as there's life, there's hope
proverbs.french: Tant qu'il y a de la vie, il y a de l'espoir
proverbs.meaning: Never lose hope, even in challenging situations.

proverbs.italian: Non si può avere la botte piena e la moglie ubriaca
proverbs.english: You can't have a barrel full of wine and a drunk wife simultaneously
proverbs.french: On ne peut pas avoir le beurre et l'argent du beurre
proverbs.meaning: Sometimes you must sacrifice one thing to gain another.

proverbs.italian: Chi nasce tondo non può morir quadrato
proverbs.english: Those who are born round can't die square
proverbs.french: Chien qui naît rond ne meurt pas carré
proverbs.meaning: Breaking bad habits is difficult and can be nearly impossible.

proverbs.italian: A caval donato non si guarda in bocca
proverbs.english: Don't look a gift horse in the mouth
proverbs.french: À cheval donné, on ne regarde pas la bouche
proverbs.meaning: Don't criticize what you've been given.

proverbs.italian: L'abito non fa il monaco
proverbs.english: You can't judge a book by its cover
proverbs.french: L'habit ne fait pas le moine
proverbs.meaning: Don't make assumptions based on appearances alone.

proverbs.italian: Il fine giustifica i mezzi
proverbs.english: The end justifies the means
proverbs.french: La fin justifie les moyens
proverbs.meaning: If the goal is noble, one shouldn't worry about the methods used.

proverbs.italian: Meglio tardi che mai
proverbs.english: Better late than never
proverbs.french: Mieux vaut tard que jamais
proverbs.meaning: It's better to complete something late than not do it at all.

proverbs.italian: Le bugie hanno le gambe corte
proverbs.english: Lies have short legs
proverbs.french: Les mensonges ont les jambes courtes
proverbs.meaning: Lies can't travel far or last long.

proverbs.italian: Ogni lasciata è persa
proverbs.english: Everything left is lost
proverbs.french: Ce qui est laissé est perdu
proverbs.meaning: Seize opportunities when they arise.

proverbs.italian: Parlare come un libro stampato
proverbs.english: To speak like a printed book
proverbs.french: Parler comme un livre imprimé
proverbs.meaning: Someone who uses language correctly and appropriately.

proverbs.italian: Piove sempre sul bagnato
proverbs.english: It always rains on the wet
proverbs.french: Il pleut toujours sur le mouillé
proverbs.meaning: Misfortune (or good fortune) tends to follow the unlucky (or lucky).

proverbs.italian: Occhio non vede, cuore non duole
proverbs.english: What the eye doesn't see, the heart doesn't grieve
proverbs.french: Ce que l'œil ne voit pas, le cœur ne souffre pas
proverbs.meaning: If you're unaware of something undesirable, you won't suffer from it.

proverbs.italian: La notte porta consiglio
proverbs.english: Night brings advice
proverbs.french: La nuit porte conseil
proverbs.meaning: Sleep on challenging decisions and make choices with a clear mind.

proverbs.italian: O mangi questa minestra o salti questa finestra
proverbs.english: Either eat this soup or jump out of this window
proverbs.french: Il faut en passer par là
proverbs.meaning: Sometimes you must choose between accepting a situation or facing consequences.

proverbs.italian: Chi si fa i fatti suoi campa 100 anni
proverbs.english: Who wants to live longer should mind his own business
proverbs.french: Qui s'occupe de ses affaires vit cent ans
proverbs.meaning: Staying out of other people's affairs leads to a happier, longer life.

proverbs.italian: Paese che vai usanze che trovi
proverbs.english: Every country you visit has different customs
proverbs.french: À chaque pays ses coutumes
proverbs.meaning: Promotes tolerance and understanding of other cultures.

proverbs.italian: Meglio soli che male accompagnati
proverbs.english: Better alone than in bad company
proverbs.french: Mieux vaut être seul que mal accompagné
proverbs.meaning: It's better to be alone than with someone who negatively impacts you.

proverbs.italian: De gustibus non disputandum est
proverbs.english: Tastes differ
proverbs.french: Des goûts et des couleurs, on ne discute pas
proverbs.meaning: Respect and be tolerant of others' preferences.

proverbs.italian: Tentar non nuoce
proverbs.english: There's no harm in trying
proverbs.french: Il n'y a pas de mal à essayer
proverbs.meaning: Valuable lessons can be learned even if an attempt fails.

proverbs.italian: L'ospite è come il pesce dopo tre giorni puzza
proverbs.english: A guest is like a fish that, after three days, stinks
proverbs.french: Le poisson et l'hôte deviennent mauvais au bout de trois jours
proverbs.meaning: Having guests stay for too long can become overwhelming.

proverbs.italian: Non dire gatto se non ce l'hai nel sacco
proverbs.english: Don't say cat if you don't have it in the bag
proverbs.french: Il ne faut pas vendre la peau de l'ours avant de l'avoir tué
proverbs.meaning: Don't count your chickens before they hatch.

proverbs.italian: Una buona giornata comincia al mattino
proverbs.english: A good day starts in the morning
proverbs.french: Une bonne journée commence le matin
proverbs.meaning: Starting each day positively sets the tone for the rest of the day.

proverbs.italian: Chi ben comincia è a metà dell'opera
proverbs.english: He who begins well is half done
proverbs.french: Bien commencer est la moitié de l'œuvre
proverbs.meaning: A strong beginning lays the foundation for successful completion.

proverbs.italian: L'erba del vicino è sempre più verde
proverbs.english: The grass isn't always greener on the other side
proverbs.french: L'herbe du voisin est toujours plus verte
proverbs.meaning: We often believe others are in a better situation, even if that's not true.

proverbs.italian: Mal comune mezzo gaudio
proverbs.english: Shared trouble, shared joy
proverbs.french: Mal partagé est à moitié guéri
proverbs.meaning: Sharing your problems makes them seem less daunting.

proverbs.italian: Troppi cuochi guastano la cucina
proverbs.english: Too many cooks spoil the broth
proverbs.french: Trop de cuisiniers gâtent la sauce
proverbs.meaning: Involving too many people in a task results in confusion.

proverbs.italian: Chi ha tempo non aspetti tempo
proverbs.english: Who has time shouldn't wait for time
proverbs.french: Qui a temps n'attende pas
proverbs.meaning: Don't procrastinate; tackle tasks today instead of putting them off.

proverbs.italian: Il mattino ha l'oro in bocca
proverbs.english: The morning has gold in its mouth
proverbs.french: Le matin a de l'or dans la bouche
proverbs.meaning: Start your day early to make the most of it.

proverbs.italian: Chi va piano, va sano e va lontano
proverbs.english: He who goes slowly, goes safely and goes far
proverbs.french: Qui va doucement, va sainement et va loin
proverbs.meaning: Patience and perseverance lead to long-term success.

proverbs.italian: Mangia bene, ridi spesso, ama molto
proverbs.english: Eat well, laugh often, love much
proverbs.french: Mange bien, ris souvent, aime beaucoup
proverbs.meaning: Enjoy life's simple pleasures for a fulfilling and joyful life.

proverbs.italian: Forza e coraggio che la vita è un passaggio
proverbs.english: Strength and courage, for life is a passage
proverbs.french: Force et courage, car la vie est un passage
proverbs.meaning: Face life's challenges with resilience and determination.

proverbs.italian: Ride bene chi ride ultimo
proverbs.english: He who laughs last laughs best
proverbs.french: Qui rit le dernier rit le mieux
proverbs.meaning: True victory is often determined at the end.

proverbs.italian: Ogni scarrafone è bello a mamma soja
proverbs.english: Every cockroach is beautiful to its mother
proverbs.french: Chaque cafard est beau à sa mère
proverbs.meaning: Someone excessively proud of their creations, no matter how flawed.

proverbs.italian: Chi dorme non piglia pesci
proverbs.english: Those who sleep don't catch fish
proverbs.french: Qui dort n'attrape pas de poisson
proverbs.meaning: Success requires effort and proactiveness.

proverbs.italian: Tra il dire e il fare c'è di mezzo il mare
proverbs.english: Talk is cheap
proverbs.french: Entre dire et faire, il y a la mer
proverbs.meaning: Actions speak louder than words.

proverbs.italian: L'appetito vien mangiando
proverbs.english: Appetite comes with eating
proverbs.french: L'appétit vient en mangeant
proverbs.meaning: Motivation grows as you engage in an activity.

proverbs.italian: A volte non si può avere tutto
proverbs.english: Sometimes you can't have it all
proverbs.french: Parfois on ne peut pas tout avoir
proverbs.meaning: You cannot always obtain everything you desire.

proverbs.italian: La vita è fatta a scale: c'è chi scende e c'è chi sale
proverbs.english: Life is like a staircase: some go up, and some go down
proverbs.french: La vie est comme un escalier: certains montent, d'autres descendent
proverbs.meaning: Everyone experiences successes and failures.

proverbs.italian: Chi non lavora, non fa l'amore
proverbs.english: He who does not work does not make love
proverbs.french: Qui ne travaille pas ne fait pas l'amour
proverbs.meaning: Hard work is essential for success in all areas of life.

proverbs.italian: Chi trova un amico, trova un tesoro
proverbs.english: He who finds a friend finds a treasure
proverbs.french: Qui trouve un ami trouve un trésor
proverbs.meaning: True friends are invaluable and should be cherished.

proverbs.italian: Chi non risica non rosica
proverbs.english: He who does not risk does not get the rose
proverbs.french: Qui ne risque rien n'a rien
proverbs.meaning: Take risks and be courageous in uncertainty.

proverbs.italian: Chi troppo vuole nulla stringe
proverbs.english: Grasp all, lose all
proverbs.french: Qui trop embrasse mal étreint
proverbs.meaning: Focusing on too many goals can lead to failure.

proverbs.italian: Non tutto quel che luccica è oro
proverbs.english: All that glitters is not gold
proverbs.french: Tout ce qui brille n'est pas or
proverbs.meaning: Not everything that seems valuable is genuine.

proverbs.italian: Chi la dura, la vince
proverbs.english: The one who perseveres wins it
proverbs.french: Qui persévère gagne
proverbs.meaning: Persistence and determination lead to success.

proverbs.italian: La calma è la virtù dei forti
proverbs.english: Calmness is the virtue of the strong
proverbs.french: Le calme est la vertu des forts
proverbs.meaning: True strength is shown through maintaining composure.

proverbs.italian: Il mondo è bello perché è vario
proverbs.english: The world is beautiful because it's varied
proverbs.french: Le monde est beau parce qu'il est varié
proverbs.meaning: Diversity and differences make life exciting and beautiful.
